patent.title: A Device to Collect Breath Data from Type-2 Diabetes Patients in Real-Life Situations
patent.abstract: A device configured to determine a level or concentration of a disease-related analyte in a person's blood from volatile analytes in the person's breath. Includes one or more sensors, a microcontroller, a transmitter, a battery, and a housing with an exhalation port.

journal.title: At-Home Breath Data Collection for Signatures of Type 2 Diabetes: A Pilot Clinical Study
journal.abstract: Investigates VOCs in breath as non-invasive biomarkers for monitoring blood glucose in Type-2 diabetes patients. A pilot clinical study with six patients using a custom sensor device achieved 85% and 82% accuracy with SVM and Random Forest models respectively.

journal.title: Microcontroller Implementation of Support Vector Machine for Detecting Blood Glucose Levels Using Breath VOCs
